name: File Canary (FIM)
description: Honeypot and File Integrity Monitor. Watches files/directories for unauthorized modifications.

documentation.summary: The File Canary acts as both a Honeypot and a File Integrity Monitor (FIM). It watches a specified directory or file on the host machine for unauthorized access, modifications, or encryption attempts (e.g., Ransomware spreading across the LAN).

sections.title: Features
content: Context-Aware Lures: Automatically generates realistic trap files based on detected host services (database backups, web config files, etc.).
Safe Permissions Handling: Uses Access Control Lists (ACLs) to securely read target directories without altering original host ownership.
Real-time Alerting: Instant telemetry transmission on file access, modification, or deletion.

sections.title: Security Architecture
content: Global Sandbox Baseline: Enforced by the HoneyWire Hub. Drops ALL default Linux kernel capabilities, enforces a read-only filesystem, and applies strict log rotation.
Execution Privilege: Runs completely unprivileged as UID 65532 to prevent system-level modifications even in the event of a breach.
Required Capabilities: DAC_OVERRIDE, operates purely through monitored Volume Mounts.

heuristics.recommendation_reason: Application runtime detected. Deploying a file integrity monitor is highly recommended to catch suspicious canary file interactions or ransomware activity.

env_vars.name: HW_HUB_ENDPOINT
env_vars.description: The URL of your central HoneyWire Hub.

env_vars.name: HW_SENSOR_ID
env_vars.description: Unique identifier for this specific trap.

env_vars.name: HW_SEVERITY
env_vars.description: Alert severity sent to the Hub.

env_vars.name: HW_ALERT_ON_OPEN
env_vars.description: Generate alerts when a file is simply read or opened (noisier).

env_vars.name: HW_DECOY_FILES
env_vars.description: Comma-separated list of absolute file paths to deploy and monitor.
env_vars.default: {{ if .HasWeb }}/var/www/html/.backup-config.php{{ else if .HasDB }}/var/lib/db/dump.sql.bak{{ else }}/opt/.env.backup{{ end }}{{ if gt (len .MatchedServices) 1 }},/home/admin/vpn_keys.txt{{ end }}
